task_completion.description: Sygic GPS Navigation application is launched
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen shows the Sygic GPS Navigation interface, such as its map view, search bar, or main menu.

task_completion.description: The process to set or edit the 'Work' location is initiated
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen shows a menu, favorites list, or dashboard where the agent is interacting with the 'Work' location option (e.g., tapping 'Add Work' or 'Set Work address').

task_completion.description: The agent searches for the World Trade Center
task_completion.visual_evidence: The search bar contains the text 'World Trade Center' (and optionally 'New York'), with search results visible.

task_completion.description: The correct World Trade Center location in New York is selected
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map view or location details screen displays the 'World Trade Center' in New York City/Lower Manhattan as the active selection.

task_completion.description: The location is successfully saved and displayed as 'Work'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A confirmation screen, favorites list, or main menu clearly shows 'Work' associated with 'World Trade Center' or its specific address in New York.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the graphical user interface of the application
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show standard Android/iOS UI interactions (taps, keyboard input, menus) rather than terminal emulators or command-line interfaces.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual Sygic GPS Navigation application
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI elements, map styling, and branding match Sygic GPS Navigation, not a generic map app, web browser, or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply type the success criteria into a text field to fake completion
integrity.visual_evidence: The text 'World Trade Center' appears in legitimate search results, map labels, or saved location UI elements, not just typed into an unrelated text box or notepad.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target location is the 'World Trade Center' in 'New York' (Lower Manhattan). Visual confirmation requires seeing 'World Trade Center' (and ideally 'New York') explicitly saved or set as the 'Work' location within the Sygic app's UI. The approximate coordinates are 40.71 N, 74.01 W, but verifying the correct name and city in the app's 'Work' configuration is the primary indicator of success.
